disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Complete loss of protective sensation in a long-standing diabetic — the deep plantar wound is entirely painless and was discovered incidentally, indicating advanced peripheral neuropathy
trigger_question_examples: Does the wound hurt him at all, or can he feel his feet normally?
Can he feel his feet — any numbness or loss of sensation?
How did he not notice a wound like that — does he have feeling in his feet?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it doesn't hurt him even a little bit. His feet have been numb for years — the doctors said the diabetes damaged the nerves. He genuinely couldn't feel it, that's why he had no idea it was there.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Deep plantar ulcer weeping malodorous seropurulent discharge — signs of an established local wound infection
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe what's coming out of the wound and how it looks?
Is there any discharge, pus or smell from the sore?
What colour is the fluid, and does the wound look deep?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There's a yellowish, cloudy fluid oozing out and it smells really foul. The sore looks deep — like a proper hole, not just a graze — and the fluid keeps weeping onto his sock.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Localised erythema extending roughly 1 cm from the wound edge — must be monitored for ascending cellulitis
trigger_question_examples: Is the skin around the wound red, and if so how far does the redness spread?
Is there any redness spreading up the foot or ankle?
Does the redness stay near the sore or is it travelling up the foot or leg?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There's a bit of redness right around the sore, maybe a centimetre out. It's not going up his foot or his leg or anything — it's just around the edge of the hole.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pertinent negative — no fever, rigors, confusion or systemic illness; currently no clinical features of systemic sepsis
trigger_question_examples: Has he had any fever, chills, shakes or shivering?
Is he confused, drowsy or unwell in himself in any way?
Any temperature, sweats or shaking that you've noticed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that. He hasn't had a fever or the shakes, he's not confused, he's completely with it and seems fine in himself. That's what surprised me given how bad the foot looks.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Long-standing poorly controlled type 2 diabetes with poor medication adherence — impaired immunity and wound healing, key risk modifier
trigger_question_examples: How well controlled is his diabetes and how does he manage his medications?
Does he take his diabetes tablets and insulin regularly?
How have his blood sugars been lately?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His diabetes has never been well controlled really. He forgets his metformin and misses his insulin a lot, and his sugars on the meter have been high lately. He's had it for about 15 years.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Ongoing unprotected weight-bearing — patient continues walking barefoot on the insensate foot, driving repetitive silent trauma
trigger_question_examples: Is he still walking on that foot, and does he wear shoes indoors?
Has he been putting weight on the foot or walking barefoot?
What footwear does he use around the house?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, he walks around the house barefoot all the time and he's been walking on it normally — he doesn't feel it so he doesn't think to protect it.
